hero.description: I build full-stack solutions with Angular, NestJS, TypeScript and PostgreSQL. Real experience building commercial systems for real users. Based in Sucre, Bolivia.

about.title: About Me
bio: I'm Manuel Adolfo Soto, a Full Stack developer and last semester student of Computer Engineering at USFX, Sucre, Bolivia.

Since 2024 I've been working as a Full Stack intern at Afer Bolivia, building an e-commerce system for home appliances. I've worked on role-based authentication, CRUDs, inventory, orders, admin panel and reports, collaborating with a multidisciplinary team of design, systems, commercial and economics.
I'm currently completing my mandatory university internship and studying intensive English (A1 level). I'm looking for remote work opportunities to keep growing professionally.

items.title: Full Stack Intern
items.description: Active participation in the complete development of an e-commerce system for home appliances. Full stack development with Angular and NestJS, including role-based authentication, product CRUD, inventory, order management, admin panel, reports and user/client interfaces. Multidisciplinary teamwork with design (Figma), systems, commercial and economics departments. Direct coordination for inventory management, sales and system improvements. Project nearing deployment, replacing a previous system.

post.title: JWT Authentication with roles in NestJS
post.intro: At Afer Bolivia I implemented a role-based authentication system (admin, user, client) using JWT in NestJS. Each role has specific permissions over resources like products, orders and reports.

sections.heading: Role structure
sections.body: I defined a role enum (admin, user, client) and a custom guard that checks the authenticated user's role before allowing access to each endpoint. This allows reusing the same logic across all controllers.

sections.heading: Token generation
sections.body: On login, the server validates credentials and generates a JWT with the user's ID and role. The token is sent to the frontend (Angular) which stores it and attaches it to every request.

sections.heading: Route protection
sections.body: I used NestJS guards with custom decorators to protect routes by role. For example, only admin can access the reports panel, while client can view their order history.

post.conclusion: This system taught me the importance of designing security from the start and how NestJS makes it easy with clean, reusable guards and decorators.
